rankings.title: Cap On, Cap Off: The Symmetric Credit Rationing Experiment from Kenya's Interest Rate Ceiling (2016-2019)
conditions: obtaining true bank-level supervisory data rather than tier aggregates
replacing tier DiD with a sharper pre-cap exposure measure
showing repeal results are not driven by COVID timing

Cap On, Cap Off: The Symmetric Credit Rationing Experiment from Kenya's Interest Rate Ceiling
conditions: demonstrating parallel pre-trends between Tier 1

rankings.title: Does Health System Capacity Determine Who Benefits from Free Maternity? County-Level Evidence from Kenya's 2013 Devolution
conditions: using retrospective birth histories to create a true event-time design
sharpening attribution between devolution
fee abolition
stress-testing against county-specific trend confounding
